data.title: CapAllianz Holds AGM in Singapore, Affirms Governance and Voting Procedures
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

CapAllianz Holdings Limited ( (SG:594) ) just unveiled an announcement.

CapAllianz Holdings Limited held its Annual General Meeting on 23 December 2025 in Singapore, with Non-Executive Chairman and Independent Director Mr. Songtao Jia presiding and the requisite quorum confirmed. The meeting followed standard corporate governance procedures, including taking the AGM notice as read with shareholder consent, providing shareholders an opportunity to submit questions in advance (none were received by the deadline), and conducting all resolutions by poll in accordance with the SGX Catalist Rules, underscoring the company’s adherence to regulatory and shareholder engagement requirements.

More about CapAllianz Holdings Limited

CapAllianz Holdings Limited is a Singapore-incorporated company listed on the Catalist board of the Singapore Exchange. The group operates as an investment holding company with interests that typically span energy and related sectors, and is subject to the governance and compliance requirements of the SGX Catalist Rules.

Current Market Cap: S$12.21M

data.title: Guangdong–Hong Kong Greater Bay Area Holdings Wins Strong Support for New Share Award Scheme
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Guangdong – Hong Kong Greater Bay Area Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1396) ) has issued an announcement.

Guangdong – Hong Kong Greater Bay Area Holdings Ltd. reported that shareholders overwhelmingly approved the adoption of a new share award scheme and related mandates at an extraordinary general meeting held on 22 January 2026. All ordinary resolutions, including the overall scheme mandate limit and a specific sub-limit for service providers, passed with 99.95% of votes in favour, with all directors attending and Computershare Hong Kong Investor Services Ltd. acting as scrutineer. The strong backing for the equity-based incentive plan gives the board a clear mandate to use share awards as a tool for aligning interests with employees and service providers and may support the company’s efforts to attract, retain and motivate key talent going forward.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1396) stock is a Hold with a HK$6.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Guangdong – Hong Kong Greater Bay Area Holdings Ltd. stock, see the HK:1396 Stock Forecast page.

More about Guangdong – Hong Kong Greater Bay Area Holdings Ltd.

Guangdong – Hong Kong Greater Bay Area Holdings Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and traded on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong under stock code 1396. The announcement notes its current board structure, comprising executive and independent non-executive directors, but does not provide details of its specific industry, business lines or principal products and services.

Average Trading Volume: 2,278,407

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: Cyviz Wins Three Boardroom Upgrade Contracts in Saudi Arabia and India
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Cyviz AS ( (DE:8P9) ) has issued an announcement.

Cyviz AS has secured three new contracts to upgrade existing boardroom and collaboration environments in Saudi Arabia and India, reinforcing its role as a specialist in high-end meeting and control solutions. The deals include an upgrade of boardroom and auditorium solutions for a leading developer of a major financial district in Saudi Arabia, delivered with a regional partner, and two boardroom upgrade projects in India for an existing Fortune 500 client in consulting and technology services. While the total contract value remains undisclosed, the projects, scheduled for delivery in the first quarter of 2026, are aimed at improving efficiency and user experience through enhanced Cyviz collaboration technology, underscoring the company’s growing footprint in key emerging and enterprise markets.

More about Cyviz AS

Cyviz AS is a global technology provider specializing in advanced conference and control rooms, as well as command and experience centers. Since 1998, the company has focused on next-generation collaboration rooms that deliver inclusive meeting experiences for in-person and remote participants, serving global enterprises and governments with high demands for user-friendliness, decision support, security and quality. Cyviz’s platform for managing and controlling systems and resources across organizations has made it a preferred supplier for customers with complex collaboration needs, and the company is listed on Euronext Growth on Oslo Børs under the ticker CYVIZ.

Average Trading Volume: 7,322

Current Market Cap: NOK503.5M

data.title: Lloyds Banking Group to Redeem €500m Senior Floating-Rate Notes One Year Early
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Lloyds Banking ( (GB:LLOY) ) has shared an announcement.

Lloyds Banking Group has exercised its call option to redeem in full its €500 million Floating Rate Senior Unsecured Callable Notes due 2027 on 5 March 2026, one year ahead of their scheduled maturity. The bank confirmed that all conditions for early redemption have been satisfied and that noteholders are not required to take any action, with the securities to be repaid at their early redemption amount plus any accrued interest, a move that forms part of the group’s ongoing capital and liability management strategy.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:LLOY) stock is a Hold with a £106.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Lloyds Banking stock, see the GB:LLOY Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GB:LLOY is a Outperform.

However, challenges in financial performance, particularly in cash flow and leverage, slightly offset these positives. The valuation is fair, with a reasonable P/E ratio and attractive dividend yield.

Lloyds Banking Group is a major UK-based financial services institution, primarily operating in retail and commercial banking, wealth management, and insurance. It issues a range of debt instruments in the capital markets to support its funding needs and balance sheet management, including senior unsecured notes aimed at institutional fixed income investors.

ValueMax Group Ltd. ( (SG:T6I) ) has provided an update.

ValueMax Group Limited has increased its issued share capital from 940,868,539 to 940,883,339 ordinary shares after the exercise of 14,800 warrants at an exercise price of S$0.36 per share on 22 January 2026. The newly issued shares, which rank pari passu with existing shares, will be listed and quoted on the Singapore Exchange on 26 January 2026, while 6,921,970 warrants remain outstanding and exercisable at S$0.36 until their expiry on 14 September 2026, indicating only a marginal dilution for existing shareholders at this stage and leaving room for further capital inflows if more warrants are exercised before expiry.

The latest announcement is out from Applied Development Holdings ( (HK:0519) ).

Novautek Technologies Group has announced the successful deployment of its intelligent transport robot in Hong Kong public housing estates, where it is being used for smart logistics tasks including automated bin collection and other daily-life applications. data.title: Kingfisher Confirms Blind Copper-Gold Porphyry Discovery at HWY 37 Project
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Kingfisher Metals ( (TSE:KFR) ) just unveiled an update.

Kingfisher Metals has confirmed the discovery of a previously unrecognized, blind copper-gold porphyry system beneath the Hank epithermal gold-silver zone at its HWY 37 Project in British Columbia’s Golden Triangle, highlighted by drillhole HW-25-011 intersecting 425 metres grading 0.40% copper equivalent. The mineralization remains open in all directions, geophysical data suggest a potentially large disseminated sulfide body, and the company is planning aggressive follow-up drilling in a sizeable 2026 target area, underscoring the prospect of a large-tonnage Cu-Au system that could materially enhance the project’s scale and strategic significance.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:KFR) stock is a Hold with a C$0.43 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Kingfisher Metals stock, see the TSE:KFR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TSE:KFR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TSE:KFR is a Neutral.

The score is primarily constrained by weak financial performance (no revenue, ongoing losses, and consistent negative free cash flow) despite a low-debt, equity-supported balance sheet. Technicals are constructive with price above major moving averages, but overbought momentum indicators temper the near-term setup. Valuation remains challenging due to negative earnings and no dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on TSE:KFR stock, click here.

More about Kingfisher Metals

Kingfisher Metals Corp. is a mineral exploration company focused on copper, gold and silver projects, with its flagship HWY 37 Project spanning 933 square kilometres in British Columbia’s Golden Triangle, an area known for large-scale precious and base metal deposits.

China Communications Services ( (HK:0552) ) has provided an announcement.

China Communications Services Corporation Limited has secured overwhelming shareholder approval to grant its board a general mandate to repurchase H shares, following an extraordinary general meeting and separate class meetings for domestic and H shareholders held in Beijing on 22 January 2026. With high turnout across share classes and more than two-thirds of votes in favour at each meeting, the special resolution passed comfortably, reinforcing the board’s flexibility in managing the company’s capital structure and potentially supporting shareholder value and market confidence in its Hong Kong-listed H shares.
